report_name: Hack-for-Hire Espionage Campaign Linked to BITTER APT Targets Phones in MENA Region

threat_actors.name: BITTER
threat_actors.description: A 'hack-for-hire' advanced persistent threat (APT) group linked to a widespread cyber-espionage campaign targeting journalists, activists, and officials in the MENA region and beyond. The group uses sophisticated phishing and social engineering to steal credentials and deploy mobile spyware.
threat_actors.goals: Cyber-espionage and surveillance, offered as a service to paying clients.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: The campaign sends highly targeted messages with malicious links to victims to direct them to fake login pages for credential harvesting or to malicious sites for spyware download.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Attackers used meticulously crafted fake login pages for services like iCloud and FaceTime to harvest Apple ID credentials from victims.

attack_patterns.name: User Execution
attack_patterns.description: Android users were tricked into installing spyware disguised as legitimate messaging applications.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Local System
attack_patterns.description: After compromising a device or an iCloud account, attackers collected sensitive data including messages, photos, contacts, and location data.

attack_patterns.name: Acquire Infrastructure
attack_patterns.description: The threat actors acquired and used nearly 1,500 malicious domains to support their large-scale phishing operations.

malware.name: Android Spyware
malware.description: Spyware for Android devices, disguised as legitimate messaging applications like Signal or WhatsApp (e.g., SignalUpdate.apk, WhatsApp_Secure.apk), used to record conversations, track location, access files, and exfiltrate data.

identities.name: Journalists
identities.description: Journalists located in the MENA region, Europe, and North America were targeted for surveillance by the hack-for-hire campaign.

identities.name: Human Rights Activists
identities.description: Activists located in the MENA region, Europe, and North America were targeted for surveillance by the hack-for-hire campaign.

identities.name: Government Officials
identities.description: Government officials located in the MENA region, Europe, and North America were targeted for espionage by the hack-for-hire campaign.

infrastructure.name: BITTER Phishing Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: A large-scale infrastructure of nearly 1,500 malicious domains used to host fake login pages for services like iCloud and FaceTime to harvest credentials.

indicators.name: BITTER Phishing Domain
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: BITTER APT Phishing Domain DNS Query
id: 2b8c9d0e-1a2b-4c3d-8e4f-5a6b7c8d9e0f
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to domains associated with a BITTER APT hack-for-hire campaign targeting individuals in the MENA region.
references:
  - https://www.itworldcanada.com/post/global-phishing-war-targets-smartphones-in-massive-hack-for-hire-espionage-campaign
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-14
tags:
  - attack.t1566
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.g1002
level: high
logsource:
  category: dns_query
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|contains:
      - 'icloud-login.net'
      - 'apple-support.co'
  condition: selection

indicators.name: BITTER Android Spyware APK
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: BITTER APT Android Spyware APK Creation
id: f0e9d8c7-6b5a-4d3c-2b1a-0f9e8d7c6b5a
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of APK files associated with a BITTER APT hack-for-hire campaign targeting Android users.
references:
  - https://www.itworldcanada.com/post/global-phishing-war-targets-smartphones-in-massive-hack-for-hire-espionage-campaign
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-14
tags:
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.execution
  - attack.g1002
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\SignalUpdate.apk'
      - '\WhatsApp_Secure.apk'
  condition: selection
